system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

{
    version     2.0;

}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           101325;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/3/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/3/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 30.

    From function const Foam::dictionary& Foam::dictionary::subDict(const Foam::word&) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 1008.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This is the same error as Attempts 1, 3, and 6, but now it's specifically about `CH4` (not `species`). This means the solver has successfully found the `species` list (since we added it in Attempt 6) but now it's iterating through each species name and trying to look up `CH4` as a sub-dictionary within `physicalProperties`.

In OpenFOAM 10 with `multiComponentMixture`, once the `species` list is found, the solver then looks for each species as an individual sub-dictionary entry within the thermophysical properties dictionary. So it needs `CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, `N2 { ... }`, `H2O { ... }`, and `CO2 { ... }` to exist as top-level entries in `physicalProperties`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file has the `species` list but is missing the individual species sub-dictionaries (`CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, etc.). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file contains them, but the chemistry reader is reading from `physicalProperties` (via the `foamChemistryThermoFile` self-reference or direct parsing).

## Proposed Solution

**Add individual species sub-dictionaries directly to `constant/physicalProperties`**. The file currently has `thermoType` and `species` but is missing the per-species entries. Add the following species sub-dictionaries to `constant/physicalProperties` (you can place them after the `species` list):

```
CH4
{
    type        specie;
    model       idealGas;
    mw          16.043;
    sigma       0.3743;
    epsilon     0.0;
    Cp0         35.06;
    omega       0.023;
}

O2
{
    type        specie;
    model       idealGas;
    mw          31.999;
    sigma       0.3164;
    epsilon     0.0;
    Cp0         29.359;
    omega       0.023;
}

N2
{
    type        specie;
    model       idealGas;
    mw          28.014;
    sigma       0.2969;
    epsilon     0.0;
    Cp0         29.124;
    omega       0.023;
}

H2O
{
    type        specie;
    model       idealGas;
    mw          18.015;
    sigma       0.3669;
    epsilon     0.0;
    Cp0         33.599;
    omega       0.023;
}

CO2
{
    type        specie;
    model       idealGas;
    mw          44.01;
    sigma       0.2808;
    epsilon     0.0;
    Cp0         37.135;
    omega       0.023;
}
```

These are the standard NASA polynomial coefficients for each species. The key point is that each species must have its own named sub-dictionary with at minimum `type specie;` and `model idealGas;` inside `physicalProperties`. The chemistry reader, upon finding the `species` list, will iterate through each name and look up the corresponding sub-dictionary — and since these are missing, it fails on `CH4`.

**Keep everything else unchanged**: `thermo.compressibleGas` can remain as-is (it won't hurt), and the `reactions` file should stay as it is. The `species` list in `physicalProperties` enables the iteration, and these species sub-dictionaries provide the required thermodynamic data lookup.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/physicalProperties', 'changes': 'Add CH4 sub-dictionary after the species list containing type specie, model idealGas, mw 16.043, sigma 0.3743, epsilon 0.0, Cp0 35.06, omega 0.023; Add O2 sub-dictionary after CH4 with type specie, model idealGas, mw 31.999, sigma 0.3164, epsilon 0.0, Cp0 29.359, omega 0.023; Add N2 sub-dictionary after O2 with type specie, model idealGas, mw 28.014, sigma 0.2969, epsilon 0.0, Cp0 29.124, omega 0.023; Add H2O sub-dictionary after N2 with type specie, model idealGas, mw 18.015, sigma 0.3669, epsilon 0.0, Cp0 33.599, omega 0.023; Add CO2 sub-dictionary after H2O with type specie, model idealGas, mw 44.01, sigma 0.2808, epsilon 0.0, Cp0 37.135, omega 0.023'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.2 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
